progress:
This is your first full Coach Review — we're working from your baseline assessment completed today (2026-07-04), so there are no week-over-week deltas yet. Here's where you stand against each goal:

**Strength & Muscle:** Baseline 1RMs estimated at Squat 87 kg, Deadlift 103 kg, Bench 67 kg. Working weights set at Squat 72.5 kg, Deadlift 85 kg, Bench 55 kg — all on a linear progression model adding 5 kg/session to lower body and 2.5 kg/session to upper body. If you hit 3 sessions/week consistently, you could realistically be squatting ~95 kg and deadlifting ~115 kg for working sets by week 8. Bench progress will be more conservative given the shoulder protocol.

**Cardio:** Estimated VO2 max sits at approximately 40–44 ml/kg/min — average for a 30-year-old male. 'Above average' for your age group starts around 47–50 ml/kg/min, so you have a meaningful but very achievable gap to close. Your 20-min swim baseline is 550 m at 125 bpm (Zone 2 — well placed). Sub-12-min 1.5-mile and 5k goals are longer-term targets; no run data yet, which is fine at this stage.

**Recovery:** Green light across the board. Resting HR 51 bpm (baseline 51), HRV 7-day avg 56 ms (baseline 55), sleep averaging 6.98 hrs/night (baseline 7.13 — slightly below, worth monitoring). No acute load data yet given only 3 logged activities in the window.
wins: Strong baseline numbers across the board — especially for someone coming in without structured programming. Your deadlift at 103 kg estimated 1RM and 40 push-ups post-bench are both genuinely impressive at 61.7 kg bodyweight. Full-depth squat with flat heels is an asset many lifters never achieve. Your resting HR of 51 bpm and HRV around 56 ms suggest your autonomic system is in good shape and primed to adapt. You also got your first pool session in (550 m, 21 mins, avg HR 125 bpm) — right in Zone 2, exactly where it should be. And you showed up for both a strength session and a swim on the same day (2026-07-03), which is a great sign of commitment in week one.
adjustments: A few things to dial in heading into week 2: (1) Activity volume is low — only 3 activities recorded in 14 days, and your first real structured session was today. Aim for 3–4 sessions next week per the plan. (2) Sleep is averaging ~6.98 hrs against a 7.13 hr baseline — not alarming, but hypertrophy and recovery are meaningfully impaired below 7 hrs. Try to protect sleep this week especially as training load picks up. (3) The June 28 'Cardio' session (indoor, 18.5 min, avg HR 114 bpm) had a max HR spike to 171 bpm — that's Zone 5, well above Zone 2. If that was supposed to be aerobic base work, the effort wasn't well calibrated. For pool sessions, keep avg HR in the 112–131 bpm band. (4) HRV was flagged UNBALANCED on 5 of the 14 days (June 23–27) — this may reflect life stress or disrupted sleep during that stretch. Worth keeping an eye on; if it continues once training ramps up, we may need to adjust load.
next_week_focus: Week 1 of structured programming. Target 3 sessions: two strength sessions (full-body or upper/lower as prescribed) and one dedicated pool swim. Strength session priority: get your first working sets in at the prescribed baseline weights — Squat 72.5 kg × 3×5, Deadlift 85 kg × 3×5, Bench 55 kg × 3×5. These should feel solid and repeatable, not maximal. Add 2–3 accessories per session (e.g., Romanian deadlift, dumbbell row, lat pulldown, cable lateral raises, face pulls mandatory on upper days). Pool session: 20 min continuous swim targeting avg HR 112–131 bpm — match or slightly exceed your 550 m baseline distance. Log every set and every swim so we have progression data to work with. Keep shoulder prehab (face pulls or band pull-aparts, 3×15–20) at the top of every upper-body session — non-negotiable.
cardio_notes: VO2 max estimated at 40–44 ml/kg/min (average for age 30 male; above-average threshold ~47–50). No Garmin-estimated VO2 max figure available yet — this will populate once you log more consistent cardio activities with HR data. Zone 2 volume this week: one swim session of ~21 minutes at avg HR 125 bpm qualifies as clean Zone 2 work — good. Target is building to 2×20–30 min Zone 2 swims per week to drive aerobic adaptation. Polarization check: the June 28 indoor cardio session had a max HR of 171 bpm (Zone 5) with an avg HR of 114 bpm (low Zone 2) — this uneven profile suggests either intervals or an effort that spiked hard then dropped. If it was meant to be easy aerobic work, those spikes are pulling you into the 'gray zone' (Zone 3–4) and away from true Zone 2 adaptation. For now, keep all cardio sessions as sustained Zone 2 swims (112–131 bpm average, no spikes above 140). True high-intensity intervals can be introduced later once your aerobic base is more developed. Running toward the 1.5-mile and 5k goals is on the roadmap but not yet introduced — pool first for at least 6–8 weeks given your cardio history and running preference.
